title: pseudogynoxys filicalyculata
intro: Pseudogynoxys filicalyculata, also known as Inca Berry, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Inca Berry include reducing inflammation and pain, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Pseudogynoxys filicalyculata is often used in teas, tinctures, and capsules, with recommended dosages varying depending on the specific preparation and intended use.
